heading: Powers and functions of Head Start agencies
label: 9837 Powers and functions of Head Start agencies

L. 105–285, § 109(5)1994—Subsec. (b). , amended subsec. (b) generally. Prior to amendment, subsec. (b) read as follows: “In order to be so designated, a Head Start agency must also (1) establish effective procedures by which parents and area residents concerned will be enabled to directly participate in decisions that influence the character of programs affecting their interests; (2) provide for their regular participation in the implementation of such programs; (3) provide technical and other support needed to enable parents and area residents to secure on their own behalf available assistance from public and private sources; (4) involve parents of children participating in its Head Start program in appropriate educational services (in accordance with the performance standards in effect upon or through referral of such parents to educational services available in the community) in order to aid their children to attain their full potential; (5) establish procedures to seek reimbursement, to the extent feasible, from other agencies for services for which any such other agency is responsible, which are provided to a Head Start participant by the Head Start agency; (6) provide (directly or through referral to educational services available in the community) parents of children participating in its Head Start program with child development and literacy skills training in order to aid their children to attain their full potential; and (7) consider providing services to assist younger siblings of children participating in its Head Start program to obtain health services from other sources.”Pub. (c). , struck out “schools that will subsequently serve children in Head Start programs,” after “coordinate with” and inserted “, including Even Start programs under part B of chapter 1 of title I of the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 ( et seq.),” after “other programs”.Pub. children.heading: Authority
children.label: a Authority
children.text: To be designated as a Head Start agency under this subchapter, an agency shall have authority under its charter or applicable law to receive and administer funds under this subchapter, funds and contributions from private or local public sources that may be used in support of a Head Start program, and funds under any Federal or State assistance program pursuant to which a public or private nonprofit or for-profit agency (as the case may be) organized in accordance with this subchapter, could act as grantee, contractor, or sponsor of projects appropriate for inclusion in a Head Start program. Such an agency shall also be empowered to transfer funds so received, and to delegate powers to other agencies, subject to the powers of its governing board and its overall program responsibilities. The power to transfer funds and delegate powers shall include the power to make transfers and delegations covering component projects in all cases where this will contribute to efficiency and effectiveness or otherwise further program objectives.

children.heading: Family and community involvement; family services
children.label: b Family and community involvement; family services

children.text: Provide for the regular and direct participation of parents and community residents in the implementation of the Head Start program, including decisions that influence the character of such program, consistent with paragraphs (2)(D) and (3)(C) of subsection (c).
Seek the involvement of parents, community residents, and local business in the design and implementation of the program.

children.label: A
children.text: to facilitate and seek the involvement of parents of participating children in activities designed to help such parents become full partners in the education of their children; and

children.label: B
children.text: to afford such parents the opportunity to participate in the development and overall conduct of the program at the local level, including transportation assistance as appropriate.

children.label: A
children.text: family literacy services; and

children.label: B
children.text: parenting skills training.

Offer to parents of participating children substance abuse counseling (either directly or through referral to local entities), if needed, including information on the effect of drug exposure on infants and fetal alcohol syndrome.

children.label: A
children.text: training in basic child development (including cognitive, social, and emotional development);

children.label: B
children.text: assistance in developing literacy and communication skills;

children.label: C
children.text: opportunities to share experiences with other parents (including parent-mentor relationships);

children.label: D
children.text: health services, including information on maternal depression;

children.label: E
children.text: regular in-home visitation; or

children.label: F
children.text: any other activity designed to help such parents become full partners in the education of their children.

Provide, with respect to each participating family, a family needs assessment that includes consultation with such parents (including foster parents, grandparents, and kinship caregivers, where applicable), in a manner and language that such parents can understand (to the extent practicable), about the benefits of parent involvement and about the activities described in this subsection in which such parents may choose to be involved (taking into consideration their specific family needs, work schedules, and other responsibilities).
Consider providing services to assist younger siblings of children participating in its Head Start program to obtain health services from other sources.
Perform community outreach to encourage individuals previously unaffiliated with Head Start programs to participate in its Head Start program as volunteers.

children.label: A
children.text: Inform custodial parents in single-parent families that participate in programs, activities, or services carried out or provided under this subchapter about the availability of child support services for purposes of establishing paternity and acquiring child support.

children.label: B
children.text: Refer eligible parents to the child support offices of State and local governments.

Provide to parents of limited English proficient children outreach and information, in an understandable and uniform format and, to the extent practicable, in a language that the parents can understand.
Provide technical and other support needed to enable parents and community residents to secure, on their own behalf, available assistance from public and private sources.

children.label: i
children.text: to inform the parents about their rights and responsibilities concerning the education of their children; and

children.label: I
children.text: to understand and work with schools in order to communicate with teachers and other school personnel;

children.label: II
children.text: to support the schoolwork of their children; and

children.label: III
children.text: to participate as appropriate in decisions relating to the education of their children; and

children.label: B
children.text: to take other actions, as appropriate and feasible, to support the active involvement of the parents with schools, school personnel, and school-related organizations.

Establish effective procedures for timely referral of children with disabilities to the State or local agency providing services under section 619 or part C of the Individuals with Disabilities Education Act (, 1431 et seq.), and collaboration with that agency, consistent with .20 U.S.C. 1419section 9835(d)(3) of this title

Establish effective procedures for providing necessary early intervening services to children with disabilities prior to an eligibility determination by the State or local agency responsible for providing services under section 619 or part C of such Act, consistent with .section 9835(d)(2) of this title

At the option of the Head Start agency, partner with an institution of higher education and a nonprofit organization to provide college students with the opportunity to serve as mentors or reading partners for Head Start participants.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The governing body shall have legal and fiscal responsibility for the Head Start agency.

children.label: i
children.text: Not less than 1 member shall have a background and expertise in fiscal management or accounting.

children.label: ii
children.text: Not less than 1 member shall have a background and expertise in early childhood education and development.

children.label: iii
children.text: Not less than 1 member shall be a licensed attorney familiar with issues that come before the governing body.

children.label: I
children.text: reflect the community to be served and include parents of children who are currently, or were formerly, enrolled in Head Start programs; and

children.label: II
children.text: are selected for their expertise in education, business administration, or community affairs.

children.label: v
children.text: Exceptions shall be made to the requirements of clauses (i) through (iv) for members of a governing body when those members oversee a public entity and are selected to their positions with the public entity by public election or political appointment.

children.label: vi
children.text: If a person described in clause (i), (ii), or (iii) is not available to serve as a member of the governing body, the governing body shall use a consultant, or an other individual with relevant expertise, with the qualifications described in that clause, who shall work directly with the governing body.

children.label: i
children.text: not have a financial conflict of interest with the Head Start agency (including any delegate agency);

children.label: ii
children.text: not receive compensation for serving on the governing body or for providing services to the Head Start agency;

children.label: iii
children.text: not be employed, nor shall members of their immediate family be employed, by the Head Start agency (including any delegate agency); and

children.label: iv
children.text: operate as an entity independent of staff employed by the Head Start agency.

children.label: i
children.text: such individual shall not be prohibited from serving on such body and the Head Start agency shall report such conflict to the Secretary; and

children.label: ii
children.text: if the position held as a result of public election or political appointment provides compensation, such individual shall not be prohibited from receiving such compensation.

children.label: i
children.text: have legal and fiscal responsibility for administering and overseeing programs under this subchapter, including the safeguarding of Federal funds;

children.label: ii
children.text: adopt practices that assure active, independent, and informed governance of the Head Start agency, including practices consistent with subsection (d)(1), and fully participate in the development, planning, and evaluation of the Head Start programs involved;

children.label: iii
children.text: be responsible for ensuring compliance with Federal laws (including regulations) and applicable State, tribal, and local laws (including regulations); and

children.label: I
children.text: selecting delegate agencies and the service areas for such agencies;

children.label: II
children.text: establishing procedures and criteria for recruitment, selection, and enrollment of children;

children.label: III
children.text: reviewing all applications for funding and amendments to applications for funding for programs under this subchapter;

children.label: IV
children.text: establishing procedures and guidelines for accessing and collecting information described in subsection (d)(2);

children.label: aa
children.text: the annual self-assessment and financial audit;

children.label: bb
children.text: such agency’s progress in carrying out the programmatic and fiscal provisions in such agency’s grant application, including implementation of corrective actions; and

children.label: cc
children.text: personnel policies of such agencies regarding the hiring, evaluation, termination, and compensation of agency employees;

children.label: VI
children.text: developing procedures for how members of the policy council are selected, consistent with paragraph (2)(B);

children.label: aa
children.text: approval of all major financial expenditures of the agency;

children.label: bb
children.text: annual approval of the operating budget of the agency;

children.label: cc
children.text: selection (except when a financial auditor is assigned by the State under State law or is assigned under local law) of independent financial auditors who shall report all critical accounting policies and practices to the governing body; and

children.label: dd
children.text: monitoring of the agency’s actions to correct any audit findings and of other action necessary to comply with applicable laws (including regulations) governing financial statement and accounting practices;

children.label: VIII
children.text: reviewing results from monitoring conducted under , including appropriate followup activities;section 9836a(c) of this title

children.label: IX
children.text: approving personnel policies and procedures, including policies and procedures regarding the hiring, evaluation, compensation, and termination of the Executive Director, Head Start Director, Director of Human Resources, Chief Fiscal Officer, and any other person in an equivalent position with the agency;

children.label: aa
children.text: any conflict of interest, and any appearance of a conflict of interest, by members of the governing body, officers and employees of the Head Start agency, and consultants and agents who provide services or furnish goods to the Head Start agency; and

children.label: bb
children.text: complaints, including investigations, when appropriate; and

children.label: XI
children.text: to the extent practicable and appropriate, at the discretion of the governing body, establishing advisory committees to oversee key responsibilities related to program governance and improvement of the Head Start program involved.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Consistent with paragraph (1)(E), each Head Start agency shall have a policy council responsible for the direction of the Head Start program, including program design and operation, and long- and short-term planning goals and objectives, taking into account the annual communitywide strategic planning and needs assessment and self-assessment.

children.label: i
children.text: The policy council shall be elected by the parents of children who are currently enrolled in the Head Start program of the Head Start agency.

children.label: I
children.text: parents of children who are currently enrolled in the Head Start program of the Head Start agency (including any delegate agency), who shall constitute a majority of the members of the policy council; and

children.label: II
children.text: members at large of the community served by the Head Start agency (including any delegate agency), who may include parents of children who were formerly enrolled in the Head Start program of the agency.

children.label: i
children.text: not have a conflict of interest with the Head Start agency (including any delegate agency); and

children.label: ii
children.text: not receive compensation for serving on the policy council or for providing services to the Head Start agency.

children.label: i
children.text: Activities to support the active involvement of parents in supporting program operations, including policies to ensure that the Head Start agency is responsive to community and parent needs.

children.label: ii
children.text: Program recruitment, selection, and enrollment priorities.

children.label: iii
children.text: Applications for funding and amendments to applications for funding for programs under this subchapter, prior to submission of applications described in this clause.

children.label: iv
children.text: Budget planning for program expenditures, including policies for reimbursement and participation in policy council activities.

children.label: v
children.text: Bylaws for the operation of the policy council.

children.label: vi
children.text: Program personnel policies and decisions regarding the employment of program staff, consistent with paragraph (1)(E)(iv)(IX), including standards of conduct for program staff, contractors, and volunteers and criteria for the employment and dismissal of program staff.

children.label: vii
children.text: Developing procedures for how members of the policy council of the Head Start agency will be elected.

children.label: viii
children.text: Recommendations on the selection of delegate agencies and the service areas for such agencies.

children.label: A
children.text: be elected and composed of members, consistent with paragraph (2)(B) (with respect to delegate agencies);

children.label: B
children.text: follow procedures to prohibit conflict of interest, consistent with clauses (i) and (ii) of paragraph (2)(C) (with respect to delegate agencies); and

children.label: C
children.text: be responsible for approval and submission of decisions about activities as they relate to the delegate agency, consistent with paragraph (2)(D) (with respect to delegate agencies).

children.label: A
children.text: the resolution of internal disputes, including any impasse in the governance of Head Start programs; and

children.label: B
children.text: the facilitation of meaningful consultation and collaboration about decisions of the governing body and policy council.

children.label: A
children.text: monthly financial statements, including credit card expenditures;

children.label: B
children.text: monthly program information summaries;

children.label: C
children.text: program enrollment reports, including attendance reports for children whose care is partially subsidized by another public agency;

children.label: D
children.text: monthly reports of meals and snacks provided through programs of the Department of Agriculture;

children.label: E
children.text: the financial audit;

children.label: F
children.text: the annual self-assessment, including any findings related to such assessment;

children.label: G
children.text: the communitywide strategic planning and needs assessment of the Head Start agency, including any applicable updates;

children.label: H
children.text: communication and guidance from the Secretary; and

children.label: I
children.text: the program information reports.

children.heading: Training and technical assistance
children.label: 3 Training and technical assistance
children.text: Appropriate training and technical assistance shall be provided to the members of the governing body and the policy council to ensure that the members understand the information the members receive and can effectively oversee and participate in the programs of the Head Start agency.

Conduct outreach to schools in which children participating in the Head Start program will enroll following the program, local educational agencies, the local business community, community-based organizations, faith-based organizations, museums, and libraries to generate support and leverage the resources of the entire local community in order to improve school readiness.

children.label: A
children.text: In communities where both a public prekindergarten program and a Head Start program operate, collaborate and coordinate activities with the local educational agency or other public agency responsible for the operation of the prekindergarten program and providers of prekindergarten, including outreach activities to identify eligible children.

children.label: i
children.text: share information about such children;

children.label: ii
children.text: collaborate with the teachers in such schools regarding professional development and instructional strategies, as appropriate; and

children.label: iii
children.text: ensure a smooth transition to school for such children.

Coordinate activities and collaborate with programs under the Child Care and Development Block Grant Act of 1990 [ et seq.], the agencies responsible for administering and parts B and E of title IV of the Social Security Act [ et seq., 670 et seq.], programs under subtitle B of title VII of the McKinney-Vento Homeless Assistance Act ( et seq.), programs under section 619 and part C of the Individuals with Disabilities Education Act (, 1431 et seq.), and other entities providing early childhood education and development programs or services, serving the children and families served by the Head Start agency.42 U.S.C. 9857section 5106a of this title42 U.S.C. 62042 U.S.C. 1143120 U.S.C. 1419

children.label: A
children.text: collaborating on the shared use of transportation and facilities, in appropriate cases;

children.label: B
children.text: collaborating to reduce the duplication and enhance the efficiency of services while increasing the program participation of underserved populations of eligible children; and

children.label: C
children.text: exchanging information on the provision of noneducational services to such children.

children.label: i
children.text: provide for a review of each of the activities described in clause (ii); and

children.label: I
children.text: educational activities, curricular objectives, and instruction;

children.label: II
children.text: public information dissemination and access to programs for families contacting the Head Start program or any of the preschool programs;

children.label: III
children.text: selection priorities for eligible children to be served by programs;

children.label: IV
children.text: service areas;

children.label: V
children.text: staff training, including opportunities for joint staff training on topics such as academic content standards, instructional methods, curricula, and social and emotional development;

children.label: VI
children.text: program technical assistance;

children.label: VII
children.text: provision of additional services to meet the needs of working parents, as applicable;

children.label: VIII
children.text: communications and parent outreach for smooth transitions to kindergarten as required in paragraphs (3) and (6) of ;section 9837a(a) of this title

children.label: IX
children.text: provision and use of facilities, transportation, and other program elements; and

children.label: X
children.text: other elements mutually agreed to by the parties to such memorandum;

children.label: i
children.text: where there is an absence of publicly funded preschool programs in the service area of a Head Start agency, this paragraph shall not apply; or

children.label: ii
children.text: where the appropriate local entity responsible for managing the publicly funded preschool programs is unable or unwilling to enter into such a memorandum, this paragraph shall not apply and the Head Start agency shall inform the Secretary and the State Director of Head Start Collaboration of such inability or unwillingness; and

children.label: C
children.text: be revised periodically and renewed biennially by the parties to such memorandum, in alignment with the beginning of the school year.

children.heading: Quality standards, curricula, and assessment
children.label: f Quality standards, curricula, and assessment

children.text: take steps to ensure, to the maximum extent practicable, that children maintain the developmental and educational gains achieved in Head Start programs and build upon such gains in further schooling;
establish a program with the standards set forth in , with particular attention to the standards set forth in subparagraphs (A) and (B) of such section;section 9836a(a)(1) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: promotes young children’s school readiness in the areas of language and cognitive development, early reading and mathematics skills, socio-emotional development, physical development, and approaches to learning;

children.label: B
children.text: is based on scientifically valid research and has standardized training procedures and curriculum materials to support implementation;

children.label: C
children.text: is comprehensive and linked to ongoing assessment, with developmental and learning goals and measurable objectives;

children.label: D
children.text: is focused on improving the learning environment, teaching practices, family involvement, and child outcomes across all areas of development; and

children.label: E
children.text: is aligned with the Head Start Child Outcomes Framework developed by the Secretary and, as appropriate, State early learning standards;
implement effective interventions and support services that help promote the school readiness of children participating in the program;

use research-based assessment methods that reflect the characteristics described in in order to support the educational instruction and school readiness of children in the program;section 9836a(b)(2) of this title

use research-based developmental screening tools that have been demonstrated to be standardized, reliable, valid, and accurate for the child being assessed, to the maximum extent practicable, for the purpose of meeting the relevant standards described in ;section 9836a(a)(1) of this title

adopt, in consultation with experts in child development and with classroom teachers, an evaluation to assess whether classroom teachers have mastered the functions discussed in ;section 9843a(a)(1) of this title

use the information provided from the assessment conducted under to inform professional development plans, as appropriate, that lead to improved teacher effectiveness;section 9836a(c)(2)(F) of this title

establish goals and measurable objectives for the provision of health, educational, nutritional, and social services provided under this subchapter and related to the program mission and to promote school readiness; and

develop procedures for identifying children who are limited English proficient, and informing the parents of such children about the instructional services used to help children make progress towards acquiring the knowledge and skills described in and acquisition of the English language.section 9836a(a)(1)(B) of this title

children.heading: Funded enrollment; waiting list
children.label: g Funded enrollment; waiting list
children.text: Each Head Start agency shall enroll 100 percent of its funded enrollment and maintain an active waiting list at all times with ongoing outreach to the community and activities to identify underserved populations.

children.heading: Technical assistance and training plan
children.label: h Technical assistance and training plan
children.text: In order to receive funds under this subchapter, a Head Start agency shall develop an annual technical assistance and training plan. Such plan shall be based on the agency’s self-assessment, the communitywide strategic planning and needs assessment, the needs of parents and children to be served by such agency, and the results of the reviews conducted under .section 9836a(c) of this title

children.heading: Financial management
children.label: i Financial management
children.text: In order to receive funds under this subchapter, a Head Start agency shall document strong fiscal controls, including the employment of well-qualified fiscal staff with a history of successful management of a public or private organization.
